text: A web browser sends an HTTP request to a web server. The server processes the request, accesses a database, and returns an HTTP response to the browser.
A class is a blueprint for creating objects. Each object can contain data in variables and behavior in methods.
FastAPI uses Dependency Injection to provide database connections and other dependencies to an endpoint.
A primary key uniquely identifies each record in a database table, while a foreign key creates a relationship between two tables.
The SQL query joins the users and orders tables, filters the results, and sorts them by creation date.
A router forwards packets between networks, while a switch connects devices inside the same local network.
The application connects to the server through port 8080. A firewall rule controls whether the network traffic is allowed.
Git tracks changes in source code. Developers create a branch, commit their work, and open a pull request on GitHub.
An asynchronous function can wait for a Promise without blocking the rest of the JavaScript application.
A machine learning model learns patterns from training data and uses those patterns to make predictions on new data.
HTTPS encrypts communication between the browser and the server, helping protect passwords and other sensitive information.
A stack follows the last-in, first-out principle, while a queue processes elements in first-in, first-out order.
